channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US Embassy issues warning to Americans in Trinidad and Tobago as tensions with Venezuela escalate
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Trinidad and Tobago is cautioning Americans to stay away from U.S. government facilities on the twin-island nation.
item.dc:creator: Anselm Gibbs, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukrainians disappointed after Trump-Zelenskyy meeting fails to immediately secure Tomahawk missiles
item.description: Ukrainians are expressing disappointment as the U.S. may not provide long-range Tomahawk missiles to Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel identifies remains of 10th deceased hostage handed over by Hamas
item.description: Israel received another hostage's remains from Hamas via the Red Cross, identifying the deceased as Eliyahu Margalit, who was killed on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One scandal too many forces UK monarchy to sideline Prince Andrew after years of tabloid fodder
item.description: Prince Andrew has been banished from the royal whirl after emails emerged this week showing that he had remained in contact with the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein longer than he previously admitted.
item.dc:creator: Danica Kirka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gaza famine claims face mounting scrutiny as mortality data falls far short of predictions
item.description: Experts challenge Gaza famine claims by the Integrated Food Security Phase Classification, citing insufficient mortality data and declining food prices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy security after deadly violence in Kenya, as thousands mourn former Prime Minister Raila Odinga
item.description: Heavy security has been deployed to Kenya’s western city of Kisumu before the final public viewing of the body of former Prime Minister Raila Odinga.
item.dc:creator: Evelyne Musambi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: Britain believes its experience in Northern Ireland could help achieve a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Hui And Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Deadly floods in Mexico another sign of need for improved severe weather warnings
item.description: Torrential rains in east-central Mexico last week have left at least 76 dead and raised concerns about the government's ability to alert people to severe weather.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas defends executions of Palestinians after Trump issues stern warning to terror group
item.description: After Trump’s threat, Hamas official Mohammed Nazzal defends the recent executions of Palestinians in Gaza as the group seeks to settle scores with opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hannibal Gadhafi, son of late Libyan leader, ordered released in Lebanon if he pays $11 million bail
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A Lebanese judge on Friday ordered the release of the son of Libya’s late leader Moammar Gadhafi on condition that he pay $11 million bail. Hannibal Gadhafi has been imprisoned in Lebanon for a decade without being charged.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky meets with Energy secretary, US companies ahead of visit with Trump
item.description: Ahead of his upcoming meeting with President Trump on Friday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said he met with Department of Energy Secretary Chris Wright and various American energy and defense companies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump is ramping up military pressure on Venezuela’s authoritarian leader Nicolás Maduro, raising the prospect of strikes inside the country, which would mark a major escalation from already controversial military attacks against alleged drug trafficking boats in international waters.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Peru's president refuses to resign after Gen Z protests leave at least 1 dead, 100 injured
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru's new President José Jerí refused to resign on Thursday following the death of a protester during a massive demonstration led by Gen Z activists demanding he step down.
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military drone strike on drug 'submersible' in Caribbean leaves survivors, official confirms
item.description: U.S. military drone strike destroyed a drug-smuggling submersible in the Caribbean Thursday, leaving 2 to 3 survivors in the water, a U.S. official tells Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas captors once held ordinary jobs
item.description: Israeli hostage Tal Shoham describes increased faith and gratitude after enduring torture and starvation during 500 days held by Hamas in Gaza tunnels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at the hostages whose remains are still inside Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas freed the last 20 living hostages taken during its Oct. 7, 2023, attack on southern Israel in exchange for the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Putin plan Hungary meeting on Ukraine war, Zelenskyy to visit White House Friday
item.description: President Donald Trump schedules call with Russian President Vladimir Putin as Ukraine seeks long-range Tomahawk missiles following recent Russian strikes on energy sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of last female Hamas hostage and IDF soldier handed over to Israel
item.description: Israel received two more deceased hostages' bodies, including that of Inbar Hayman, who was considered to be the last female hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Just-released hostage attends funeral of fellow soldier, whose body was among few returned from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Two hostages released by Hamas were reunited Wednesday in a Jerusalem cemetery for a final goodbye. Surrounded by hundreds of mourners, Matan Angrest, who had returned to Israel just two days earlier, stood before the freshly dug grave cradling his 22-year-old commander, Capt.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israel receives remains of 2 more hostages as military says another body was not that of a hostage
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel received the remains of two more hostages Wednesday, hours after the Israeli military said that one of the bodies previously turned over was not that of a hostage. The confusion added to tensions over the fragile truce that has paused the two-year war.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Florida doctor detained by Israel during aid mission to Gaza describes experience
item.description: Two Florida residents who were detained by Israel during an aid mission to Gaza are describing their experience in a Wednesday press conference.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: NATO allies agree to increase weapon deliveries to Ukraine
item.description: Over half of NATO allies are now part of the Prioritized Ukraine Requirements List, a program to buy U.S. weapons for the Eastern European nation.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Russia releases JFK files to Rep. Anna Paulina Luna
item.description: The transfer of the 350-page document was confirmed this week by Russia’s ambassador to the U.S. and Luna, who chairs a House panel on government secrets.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey deports peaceful Christians under guise of ‘national security’ claims watchdog
item.description: Human rights advocate warns Turkey is systematically targeting Christians for deportation, with President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan's policies facing international scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukrainian officials meet with US weapons manufacturers before Trump-Zelenskyy talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Ukrainian government delegation has met with prominent American weapons manufacturers during a U.S. visit, a senior Kyiv official said Wednesday, before President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s meeting with U.S. counterpart Donald Trump at the White House later this week.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pakistan and Taliban agree to 48-hour ceasefire after renewed fighting kills dozens
item.description: A temporary ceasefire has been agreed to after border violence escalated between Pakistan and Afghanistan over the weekend as both sides blame each other for attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Kenyan premier Raila Odinga, a key figure in African democracy efforts, dies at 80
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Raila Odinga, a former prime minister of Kenya and perennial presidential candidate whose populist campaigns challenged one-party rule, rattled authorities and gave him outsized influence on political life in the East African country, died Wednesday of a heart attack while trav
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: IDF says body turned over by Hamas doesn't match any hostages
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces announced that a corpse surrendered by Hamas was not a match for any of the hostages who are supposed to be released.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel frees some Gaza medical staff, but a prominent hospital chief remains imprisoned
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Under Gaza’s ceasefire deal, Israel freed dozens of doctors, nurses, paramedics and other medical personnel seized during raids on hospitals. But more than 100 remain in Israeli prisons, including Dr.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Snipers on stadium roof amid heavy security for Italy's win over Israel in World Cup qualifying
item.description: UDINE, Italy (AP) — The World Cup qualifier between Italy and Israel took place amid a heavy police presence that included snipers on the stadium roof.
item.dc:creator: DANIELLA MATAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American who was on death row in China details horrifying experiences
item.description: Speaking in his first television interview since being released in November 2024 as part of a prisoner swap, Swidan detailed his experience on "Elizabeth Vargas Reports.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says it has received the remains of 4 more deceased hostages as cuts to aid for Gaza loom
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas hastened Tuesday to ease the pressure on a fragile ceasefire in its war with Israel by returning the bodies of more dead hostages.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Therapist explains what Hamas hostages are feeling
item.description: Dr. Rebecca Bailey joins "Banfield" to discuss the mental process the former Hamas hostages will be going through.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Schumer, Clinton give Trump credit for Gaza ceasefire, hostage release
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and former President Clinton gave President Trump credit for the ceasefire in Gaza and the return of Israeli hostages by Hamas. “Today is a wonderful day.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois community and leaders react as Israel and Hamas exchange hostages and prisoners
item.description: In a critical day for the Middle East, Hamas released the 20 living Israeli hostages it still held and Israel released nearly 2,000 Palestinian prisoners under a breakthrough Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: Israeli, Palestinian families celebrate return of hostages, prisoners
item.description: The last remaining living Israeli hostages were released as well as more than 1,900 detained Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Israel expects hostages to be freed from Gaza 'in a few hours' as ceasefire holds
item.description: Israel said Sunday that it expected all living hostages held in Gaza to be released Monday in its breakthrough ceasefire deal with Hamas, as Palestinians awaited the release of hundreds of prisoners held in Israel and a surge of aid into the famine-stricken territory.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, SARAH EL DEEB and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
